title: vulpia ciliata subsp. ambigua
intro: Vulpia ciliata subsp. ambigua, also known as greater hair grass or vulpia, is a perennial grass species native to the temperate regions of Europe, Asia, and North Africa. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of vulpia include its ability to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. In herbal preparations, vulpia is often used as a tincture or tea, and can also be applied topically as a poultice or compress to aid in wound healing and skin conditions.
